title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. arenaria
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. arenaria, also known as kidney vetch, is a flowering plant in the legume family, commonly found in dry, sandy areas and coastal regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of kidney vetch are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Kidney vetch is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies such as creams and ointments to soothe and protect the skin.
